NSAA-2019-M1-002: Find the complete set of values of $x$ which satisfy the inequality $\frac12(3x-2) - \frac23(x-4) < x$.
NSAA-2019-M1-003: The equation gives $y$ in terms of $x$: $y=3-4\left(1-\dfrac{x}{2}\right)^2$. Which one of the following is a rearrangement for $x$ in terms of $y$?
NSAA-2019-M1-004: The resistance to the motion of a car is directly proportional to the square of the speed of the car. The car increases its speed by 20%. What is the percentage increase in the resistance to the motion of the car?
NSAA-2019-M1-005: The equation of a curve is $y = px^2 + qx$ where $p$ and $q$ are constants. The curve passes through the points $(2, 6)$ and $(4, -4)$. What is the value of $q - p$?
NSAA-2019-M1-006: Which of the following is a simplification of $4-\dfrac{x(3x+1)}{x^2(3x^2-2x-1)}$?
NSAA-2019-M1-007: The ball for a garden game is a solid sphere of volume $192\text{ cm}^3$. For the children's version of the game the ball is a solid sphere made of the same material, but the radius is reduced by 25%. What is the volume, in cm$^3$, of the children's ball?
NSAA-2019-M1-008: The diagram shows a right-angled triangle, with sides of length $x+4$, $2x+2$ and $3x$, all in cm. [DIAGRAM: right triangle labelled with sides x+4, 2x+2, 3x, not to scale]. What is the area, in cm$^2$, of the triangle?
NSAA-2019-M1-009: Given that $9^{2x-1} \times \dfrac{1}{27^{x}} = 81^{x}$, what is the value of $x$?
NSAA-2019-M1-010: PR and QS are the diagonals of a rhombus PQRS. PR = $(3x+2)$ cm, QS = $(8-2x)$ cm. The area of PQRS is $11\text{ cm}^2$. What is the difference, in cm, between the two possible lengths of PR?
NSAA-2019-M1-011: The diagram shows two congruent right-angled triangles PQR and TSR with right angles at Q and S, respectively. PQ = TS = 3 cm, QR = SR = 4 cm. PRT is a straight line. [DIAGRAM: Q and S above line PRT, forming two right triangles sharing vertex R]. What is the length, in cm, of QS?
NSAA-2019-M1-012: The total of three numbers p, q and r is 375. The ratio p:q is 5:7. The ratio q:r is 4:11. What is the value of p + r?
NSAA-2019-M1-013: The straight line P has equation $3y - 2x = 12$ and intercepts the y-axis at the point $(0, p)$. The straight line Q is parallel to P, passes through the point $(6, -1)$ and intercepts the y-axis at the point $(0, q)$. What is the value of $p - q$?
NSAA-2019-M1-014: The vertices of a rectangle have coordinates: P(4, 5), Q(4, 8), R(10, 8), S(10, 5). PQRS is transformed by a clockwise rotation of 90° about P followed by a reflection in the x-axis. What are the coordinates of the final position of R?
NSAA-2019-M1-015: Box A contains exactly 10 balls, of which 6 are red and 4 are blue. Box B contains exactly 15 balls, of which 3 are red and 12 are blue. All the balls are identical in every respect, apart from colour. One of the two boxes is chosen at random by tossing two fair coins: "If both coins show heads, box A is selected. Otherwise box B is selected." One ball is then randomly taken from the selected box. What is the probability that a red ball is taken?
NSAA-2019-M1-016: Three towns Ryeton, Tonbridge and Uphampton are represented on the diagram by the points labelled R, T and U, respectively. [DIAGRAM: points R, T, U with north direction marked at T]. The distance from Tonbridge to Ryeton is the same as the distance from Tonbridge to Uphampton. Uphampton is south of Tonbridge. Ryeton is on a bearing of 210° from Tonbridge. What is the bearing of Uphampton from Ryeton?
NSAA-2019-M1-017:
A list of five numbers has mean $x$, median $y$ and range $z$. A sixth number is added to the list. This sixth number is greater than $x$. Which of the following statements must be true?
1 The median of the six numbers cannot be one of the numbers in the list.
2 The mean of the six numbers is greater than $x$.
3 The range of the six numbers is greater than $z$.
NSAA-2019-M1-018: [DIAGRAM: square-based pyramid, base side 12 cm, vertical height h] A solid pyramid has a square base of side length 12 cm and a vertical height of h cm. The volume of the pyramid, in cm$^3$, is equal to the total surface area of the pyramid, in cm$^2$. What is the value of h? (volume of pyramid $=\frac13\times$ area of base $\times$ vertical height)
NSAA-2019-PHY-001: A transverse wave is travelling through a medium. The distance between successive wave peaks is 8.4 cm and the total distance travelled by a particle during one complete oscillation is 5.6 cm. What is the amplitude and wavelength of the wave?
NSAA-2019-PHY-002: A builder lifts bags of cement onto the back of a lorry. Each bag has a mass of 25 kg. It takes the builder 2.5 minutes to load ten bags. [DIAGRAM: bag of cement lifted from ground level 1.5 m to lorry bed at 2.0 m]. What are the total work done, T, on the ten bags and the average power required for T? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
NSAA-2019-PHY-003: [DIAGRAM: current-voltage graph for a diode, current axis 0-16 mA, voltage axis -0.6 V to 1.4 V] The diode is connected in series with a resistor and a 6.0 V battery. The current in the circuit is 8.0 mA. What is the resistance of the resistor? (Assume that the battery has negligible resistance.)
NSAA-2019-PHY-004: Two electromagnetic waves P and Q travel in a vacuum and the ratio of their wavelengths is: $\dfrac{\text{wavelength of P}}{\text{wavelength of Q}} = 1.0\times10^{8}$. Which row in the table shows the ratio of their speeds, the ratio of their frequencies, and identifies the possible natures of P and Q?
NSAA-2019-PHY-005: A block of aluminium of mass 0.80 kg, initially at a temperature of $-21°C$, is supplied with 54000 J of thermal energy. The specific heat capacity of aluminium is 900 J kg$^{-1}$ °C$^{-1}$. What is the final temperature of the block? (Assume no other transfer of energy between the block and the surroundings.)
NSAA-2019-PHY-006:
A light spring is used to support a range of loads. The spring obeys Hooke's law. The system is in equilibrium. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 The tension in the spring is directly proportional to the length of the spring.
2 The tension in the spring and the weight of the load it supports are a Newton's third law pair of forces.
3 When the extension of the spring is doubled, the energy stored in the spring increases by a factor of four.
NSAA-2019-PHY-007: A water-tight cylinder with a thin, freely moving piston contains $2.0\times10^{-3}\text{ m}^3$ of trapped air at atmospheric pressure of $1.0\times10^5$ Pa. When the cylinder is submerged in water of constant density 1000 kg m$^{-3}$, the volume of air in the cylinder decreases to $4.0\times10^{-4}\text{ m}^3$. The piston is at a depth h below the surface of the water and the water surface is open to the atmosphere. What is the depth h? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume temperature constant and air is ideal)
NSAA-2019-PHY-008: The secondary coil of an ideal, 100% efficient transformer is connected to a resistor by cables of total resistance $1500\,\Omega$. The current in the primary coil is 4.0 A. There are 240 turns in the primary coil and 4800 turns in the secondary coil. What is the power produced as heat in the cables?
NSAA-2019-PHY-009: Heat is supplied to an initially solid substance at a rate of 60 W. [DIAGRAM: graph of temperature vs time from 0-80 s, showing a rising section, then a flat plateau (melting), then rising again]. What is the mass of the substance? (specific latent heat of fusion = 100 J g$^{-1}$; assume no heat lost to surroundings)
NSAA-2019-PHY-010: Two tanks of water are connected by a solid cylindrical copper bar of length l and diameter d. The bar is insulated. One tank contains water at 90°C and the other tank contains water at temperature θ. [DIAGRAM: two tanks connected by insulated cylindrical bar]. For which of the following conditions is thermal energy conducted along the bar at the lowest rate?
NSAA-2019-PHY-011: A U-shaped permanent magnet rests on a balance. A straight, horizontal wire of length 5.0 cm is fixed in position between the poles of the magnet, perpendicular to the horizontal magnetic field. There is a current of 2.0 A in the wire and the reading on the balance is 202 g. When the direction of the 2.0 A current is reversed, the reading changes to 198 g. What is the strength of the magnetic field? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
NSAA-2019-PHY-012: The radioactive isotope X becomes the stable isotope Y after a succession of decays involving only the emission of alpha and beta ($\beta^-$) particles. During the decay of one nucleus from X to Y, a total of seven particles are emitted. It is known that more of these particles are alpha particles than beta particles. The atomic number of X is Z and the mass number of X is A. Which row in the table could give the atomic number and mass number of Y?
NSAA-2019-PHY-013: The kinetic energy of an object of mass 4.0 kg, travelling in a straight line, increases from 32 J to 200 J in 3.0 seconds due to a constant resultant force. What is the value of this resultant force?
NSAA-2019-PHY-014: In the following circuit, all five resistors have the same resistance. [DIAGRAM: circuit of five equal resistors with a voltmeter]. The reading on the voltmeter is 1.0 V. What is the voltage across the battery?
NSAA-2019-PHY-015: Two hovercraft travel horizontally in opposite directions along the same straight line. Hovercraft M: mass 1000 kg, speed 20 m/s. Hovercraft N: mass 2000 kg, speed 10 m/s. Horizontal resistive forces are negligible. The hovercraft collide and stick together. The collision lasts for 0.10 s. Just before the collision, what is the total kinetic energy and the magnitude of the total momentum of the two hovercraft, and the magnitude of the average force acting horizontally on each hovercraft during the collision?
NSAA-2019-PHY-016: [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph for a car over 85 seconds, velocity axis 0-16 m/s] What proportion of the total distance is travelled at constant velocity?
NSAA-2019-PHY-017: A block is designed with a cylindrical channel to accommodate a hot-water pipe. The block is 100 cm long and has a square cross-section of side 22.0 cm with a cylindrical hole in the middle. The diameter of the cylindrical hole is 14.0 cm and the density of the material is 0.100 g cm$^{-3}$. What is the mass of the block? (take $\pi = \frac{22}{7}$)
NSAA-2019-PHY-018: A sample initially contains equal numbers of atoms of a radioactive isotope X and a stable isotope Y. Isotope X has a half-life of 3 years and decays in a single stage to the stable isotope Y. What is the ratio (number of atoms of X):(number of atoms of Y) in the sample 6 years later?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-001:
HBr reacts with pent-2-ene in an addition reaction. Which of the following products is/are formed in the reaction?
1 CH3CHBrCH2CH2CH3
2 CH2BrCH2CH2CH2CH3
3 CH3CH2CHBrCH2CH3
NSAA-2019-CHEM-002:
In which of the following solids does the bonding consist of single covalent bonds only?
1 graphite
2 SiO2
3 Al2O3
NSAA-2019-CHEM-003: Four samples, labelled W, X, Y and Z, were investigated using paper chromatography with a solvent that caused any mixtures present to be fully separated. [DIAGRAM: chromatogram with scale 0-12 cm and spots for samples W, X, Y, Z, some samples showing multiple spots]. What is the Rf value of the spot with the strongest attraction to the mobile phase relative to the stationary phase and that is from a sample containing only one substance?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-004: A mass spectrometer measures isotope masses and abundances. [DIAGRAM: three bar charts showing relative mass vs percentage abundance for lithium isotopes (6,7), carbon isotopes (11,13,14), and oxygen isotopes (15,16,17,18,19), with some bars at ~0% abundance]. Using this data, what is the largest possible relative molar mass of lithium carbonate, Li2CO3?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-005:
The following information about metals labelled P, Q, R and S is given.
• Metals P and S can be extracted by electrolysis, but not by reaction with carbon.
• Metals Q and R can be extracted by reaction with carbon.
• Metal S forms positive ions more readily than metal P.
• Metal R reacts with the oxide of metal Q.
What is the order of reactivity of these four metals, starting with the most reactive?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-006: A simple ion of an element with atomic number x has a mass number of $(2x+2)$. The ion has a charge of $-2$. How many protons, neutrons and electrons are present in this ion?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-007: A 116 g sample of an oxide of iron contains 84 g of iron. Which of the following is the empirical formula of this oxide of iron? (Ar values: O = 16; Fe = 56)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-008:
Which of the following chemical equations represent(s) a redox reaction?
1 2Cu2+ + 4I– → 2CuI + I2
2 Al3+ + 3e– → Al
3 2Ag+ + 2OH– → Ag2O + H2O
NSAA-2019-CHEM-009: X is an anhydrous salt of iron containing one type of cation and one type of anion. An aqueous solution of X gives a white precipitate when aqueous barium chloride is added in the presence of hydrochloric acid. On adding aqueous sodium hydroxide to an aqueous solution of X, a brown precipitate formed immediately. The relative atomic mass of iron is 56, and its atomic number is 26. What is the relative molar mass of X? (Ar: C=12; N=14; O=16; S=32; Cl=35.5; Br=80)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-010: In the electrolysis of dilute sulfuric acid, hydrogen gas is formed at the negative electrode (cathode) and oxygen gas is formed at the positive electrode (anode). If 100 g of hydrogen gas is formed, what mass of oxygen gas is also formed? (Ar: H=1; O=16)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-011: 0.005 mol of a chloride of element X was dissolved in water and then reacted with excess silver nitrate solution to form a precipitate of silver chloride, AgCl. This precipitate is the only product containing chlorine. After filtering, washing and drying, the mass of the precipitate was recorded to be 1.435 g. Which of the following could be the formula of the chloride of X? (Mr AgCl = 143.5)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-012: A chemical equation that represents the reaction of phosphorus with concentrated nitric acid is: $P_4 + wHNO_3 + H_2O \to xH_3PO_4 + yNO + zNO_2$. What is the value of the sum $w+x+y+z$?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-013: A 2.40 g lump of magnesium was added to 500 cm3 of a 2.00 mol dm-3 solution of HCl in a conical flask on an electronic balance. Mass loss (= mass of H2 released) was recorded over time, giving line 1 on a graph. [DIAGRAM: mass loss vs time graph showing line 1 and line 2]. Which experiment performed under the same conditions would give line 2 (with a greater final mass loss but similar initial rate profile)? (Ar Mg=24.0)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-014: A technician needs to separate three liquids (X, Y and Z) which have been accidentally mixed together. None of the liquids react with each other. Liquid X: bp 65°C, density 0.79 g/cm3. Liquid Y: bp 51°C, density 0.68 g/cm3. Liquid Z: bp 100°C, density 1.00 g/cm3. X and Z are miscible, but Y is immiscible with both X and Z. The technician uses a separating funnel to separate the upper and lower layers. What should the technician do next to maximise separation of the three liquids?
NSAA-2019-CHEM-015: In each of the following procedures an excess of the metal is added to 1.0 dm3 of a 1.0 mol dm-3 solution of the acid: copper added to sulfuric acid; iron added to hydrochloric acid; magnesium added to sulfuric acid; zinc added to hydrochloric acid. Which row identifies combinations of metal and acid that will react and produce the largest, and the smallest, theoretical mass of anhydrous salt? (Mr: CuSO4=160; FeCl2=127; MgSO4=120; ZnCl2=136)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-016: 3.4 g of an impure sample of silicon tetrachloride is reacted with water. The mixture is filtered and the resulting solution made up to 250 cm3. SiCl4(l) + 2H2O(l) → SiO2(s) + 4HCl(aq). 12.5 cm3 of this solution is neutralised exactly by 20.0 cm3 of 0.100 mol dm-3 sodium hydroxide. What is the percentage purity of the silicon tetrachloride? (Mr SiCl4 = 170; assume the impurity does not react)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-017: The Haber process is represented by: $N_2 + 3H_2 \to 2NH_3$. What is the overall enthalpy change for the reaction? (Bond enthalpies: N≡N = 945 kJ/mol; H–H = 435 kJ/mol; N–H = 390 kJ/mol)
NSAA-2019-CHEM-018:
The diagram shows the electrolysis of molten lead(II) bromide, PbBr2, using graphite electrodes to separate the compound into its elements. [DIAGRAM: electrolysis cell with molten PbBr2 and two graphite electrodes]. Which of the following statements about this electrolysis is/are correct?
1 Lead is formed at the negative electrode.
2 Electrons flow through the external circuit away from the positive electrode towards the negative electrode.
3 Bromine molecules and lead are produced in a 2:1 molar ratio.
NSAA-2019-BIO-001:
Which of the following statements is/are correct for both bacterial cells and sperm cells?
1 The cell can divide.
2 The cell contains DNA.
3 The cell has a cell wall.
4 The cell has a nucleus.
5 The cell can carry out respiration.
NSAA-2019-BIO-002: Which one of the following statements about cells or tissues is correct?
NSAA-2019-BIO-003: The diagram represents a section of DNA, with parts labelled 1, 2 and 3. [DIAGRAM: DNA double helix section with three labelled parts: a phosphate group, a sugar, and a base]. What do the parts labelled 1, 2 and 3 represent?
NSAA-2019-BIO-004: Using the information in the table, which animal (A-F) is most at risk of extinction?
NSAA-2019-BIO-005: An ecologist used a 50 cm × 50 cm square quadrat to estimate the number of meadow buttercups in a field with an area of 50 m2. The quadrat was placed randomly ten times and buttercups counted: 3, 10, 0, 4, 21, 19, 6, 11, 15, 3. How many buttercups were there estimated to be in the 50 m2 field?
NSAA-2019-BIO-006:
A photomicrograph shows a section through the wall of a human bronchus. [DIAGRAM: photomicrograph of bronchus wall showing a ciliated/goblet cell epithelial layer and a gland-cell layer]. Layer 1: goblet cells (secrete mucus protein) and ciliated cells. Layer 2: a group of similar cells, each secreting a protein that surrounds the cells. Which of the following conclusions is/are correct?
1 The two different layers are both tissues.
2 The bronchus can be described as an organ.
3 Amino acids are found in the cytoplasm of cells in each layer.
NSAA-2019-BIO-007:
A student modelled gas movement in a leaf's air space using diffusion of an alkaline gas along a tube, timing colour change of damp litmus paper at 2 cm intervals. [DIAGRAM: apparatus with cotton wool releasing gas, litmus paper at intervals, and a graph of time to colour change vs distance]. Which of the following statements about the investigation is/are correct?
1 The dependent variable has been plotted on the x-axis.
2 If diffusion of the gas was slower, the graph line would become steeper.
3 If a more concentrated solution was used in an identical apparatus, the data would result in a line below the points plotted for the original gas.
NSAA-2019-BIO-008: A bacterium was genetically modified by inserting a human gene into a plasmid to produce a human protein. The gene contains a unique base sequence not repeated elsewhere and found in every allele of the gene. Assuming no mutations occur, how many copies of this base sequence would be found at: Stage X (one human liver cell containing the gene), Stage Y (one copy of gene removed from human cell, then inserted into one bacterial plasmid), Stage Z (purified human protein after expression)?
NSAA-2019-BIO-009:
A student set up an experiment to measure the rate of photosynthesis using pondweed, light source, and a gas-collecting measuring cylinder. [DIAGRAM: apparatus with pondweed, light source, and measuring cylinder collecting gas]. If plotted, which of the following variable pairs would give a gradient that is directly proportional to the rate of photosynthesis (all other variables constant)?
1 x-axis: time, y-axis: volume of CO2 released
2 x-axis: time, y-axis: number of gas bubbles released per minute
3 x-axis: volume of oxygen released, y-axis: time
NSAA-2019-BIO-010:
Bacteria reproduce asexually by dividing into two by binary fission. Which of the following statements is/are correct about binary fission in bacteria?
1 As it is a form of asexual reproduction, there cannot be any variation in the offspring.
2 It can lead to a repeated doubling in population size if there are no limiting factors.
3 If binary fission occurs every 20 minutes, one bacterium would become $72^2$ bacteria in 24 hours.
NSAA-2019-BIO-011:
Which of the following may stay the same when a mutation occurs in a human gene that codes for a protein?
1 the genotype of the organism's offspring
2 the phenotype of the organism
3 the sequence of amino acids in the protein
NSAA-2019-BIO-012:
A child is affected by a dominant genetic condition. All of his cells have the same genotype. The allele associated with this condition is not present in either of his parents. Which of the following statements could explain this?
1 A mutation occurred during meiosis in his father.
2 A random mutation occurred in his DNA after he was born.
3 Both of his grandmothers had the condition.
NSAA-2019-BIO-013:
Phosphatase enzymes break the bond between a phosphate group and the rest of a molecule. Phenolphthalein phosphate (PPP) is colourless and a substrate for phosphatase; removing the phosphate produces phenolphthalein, which is pink in alkaline conditions. Five tubes with equal PPP and plant phosphatase enzyme were set at different pH (3.2, 4.2, 5.2, 8.2, 9.2), incubated, then Na2CO3 added to bring pH to 9.5. Colours observed: pale pink, pink, dark pink, colourless, colourless (same immediately and after 5 more minutes). Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 Na2CO3 solution inhibited the activity of the phosphatase enzyme.
2 More substrate was produced in conditions of high acidity compared to low acidity.
3 The optimum pH for this plant phosphatase must be 5.2.
NSAA-2019-BIO-014: A gene found on the X chromosome in humans has two alleles, dominant and recessive. Individuals who have only recessive alleles have a condition that affects their eyesight. [DIAGRAM: family tree showing females without the condition, female carriers, males without the condition, and males with the condition]. If one living skin cell from each member of this family were analysed, how many copies of the recessive allele and how many copies of the dominant allele would be found in total?
NSAA-2019-BIO-015: The graphs below show the oxygen uptake before, during and after a period of strenuous physical exercise. [DIAGRAM: six graph options (A-F) each showing oxygen uptake vs time across rest-exercise-rest phases, with a different shaded area in each]. Which shaded area correctly represents the additional oxygen taken in to repay the oxygen debt acquired through anaerobic respiration?
NSAA-2019-BIO-016:
An investigation used clones of one plant. Twenty plants of the same initial height were divided into four equal groups, each kept at a different temperature but with equal soil/nutrients, equal water, and the same light intensity, grown for six months. [DIAGRAM: bar chart of mean plant height vs temperature for pots 1-4, showing pot 3 and pot 4 with equal mean height]. Which of the following statements could explain the results?
1 The difference in mean height between plants in pot 1 and plants in pot 2 could be due to the environment.
2 The mean height of plants in pot 4 equals that of plants in pot 3 because of another environmental factor in addition to temperature.
3 The mean height of plants in pot 3 equals that of plants in pot 4 because of the genotype of the plants.
NSAA-2019-BIO-017:
An investigation was carried out on the effect of substrate concentration on an enzyme-controlled reaction. Four substrate concentrations were tested (0.25, 0.50, 1.0, 5.0 mmol dm-3), and product concentration was measured over time (0-120 s). [DIAGRAM: graph of product concentration vs time for four substrate concentrations]. Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1 As substrate concentration increases, the time taken to produce 0.2 arbitrary units of product increases.
2 Doubling the substrate concentration always doubles the rate of the reaction.
3 The average rate of reaction for a substrate concentration of 1.0 mmol dm-3 is 0.005 arbitrary units per second.
NSAA-2019-BIO-018:
The sequence of a coding section of DNA is shown below:
CTAGTCTGGTGGCTACGTCCTCCTCTACTATGGCTAGTCGTCTGGTGGCTA
The number of each type of amino acid coded for by this sequence is: Arg=1, Leu=6, Pro=2, Trp=5, Val=3. What is the correct sequence of amino acids coded for by this sequence of DNA bases?
NSAA-2019-M1-019: Find the area of the shape bounded by the four lines: $2y + x = 4$, $x = -6$, $x = 0$, $y = 0$.
NSAA-2019-M1-020: The curve $y = x^3 + px^2 + qx + r$ has a local maximum when $x=-1$ and a local minimum when $x=3$. What is the value of $p$?
NSAA-2019-PHY-019: A hydroelectric power station uses the water in a reservoir to power the generators. The water falls through a vertical height of 150 m to the turbines which power the generators. The efficiency of the power station is 90% and the output power is 1800 MW. (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; density of water = 1000 kg m$^{-3}$) What volume of water passes through the turbines in one minute?
NSAA-2019-PHY-020: A car P of mass 1000 kg is travelling north at 30 m/s along a straight, horizontal road when it hits another car Q directly ahead of P, travelling in the same direction. Car Q has mass 500 kg and is travelling at 20 m/s. The collision lasts 0.20 s and immediately after the collision car Q is moving north at 30 m/s. What is the speed of P immediately after the collision and the size of the average resultant force acting on Q during the collision? (Assume no external forces act during the collision.)
NSAA-2019-M1-021: It is given that $7\cos x + \tan x \sin x = 5$ where $0° < x < 90°$. What are the possible values of $\tan x$?
NSAA-2019-PHY-021: A metal wire of length 0.50 m has a uniform cross-sectional area of $4.0\times10^{-7}\text{ m}^2$. There is a current of 4.0 A in the wire. What is the potential difference across the ends of the wire? (resistivity = $1.6\times10^{-7}\,\Omega$m)
NSAA-2019-M1-022: An equilateral triangle of side 8 cm is drawn so that its vertices lie on the circumference of a circle. [DIAGRAM: equilateral triangle inscribed in circle with three circular segments shaded outside the triangle]. What is the total of the three areas shaded in the diagram, in cm$^2$?
NSAA-2019-PHY-022: A uniform square trap door of side 0.80 m and mass 14 kg has a smooth hinge at one edge and is held open at an angle of 30° to the horizontal. It is supported by a single rigid rod placed so that it meets the surface of the trap door at 90°, at a distance 0.10 m from the top edge of the trap door. [DIAGRAM: trapdoor at 30° to horizontal, hinge at bottom, supporting rod perpendicular to door surface 0.10 m from top edge]. What is the normal contact force exerted on the trap door by the rod? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
NSAA-2019-M1-023: Which one of the following is the real solution of the equation $3\times5^{2x+1}-5^{x}-2=0$?
NSAA-2019-PHY-023: Two small loudspeakers are placed side by side 30 cm apart. They are connected to the same signal generator so that they emit sound of frequency 400 Hz in phase with one another. The sounds both reach a microphone placed 40 cm directly in front of one of the two loudspeakers. [DIAGRAM: two speakers 30 cm apart, microphone 40 cm from the nearer speaker]. What is the phase difference between waves from the loudspeakers as they arrive at the microphone? (speed of sound = 320 m/s)
NSAA-2019-M1-024: For a particular function f(x), it is given that: $2\int_{-2}^{2}f(x)\,dx + \int_{2}^{4}f(x)\,dx = 4$ and also: $5\int_{-2}^{2}f(x)\,dx - \int_{-2}^{4}f(x)\,dx = 7$. Find the value of $\int_{2}^{4}f(x)\,dx$.
NSAA-2019-PHY-024: An astronaut on the Moon throws a ball vertically upwards. The ball has a mass of 2.0 g and is thrown upwards at 80 m/s. What is the maximum height gained by the ball? (gravitational field strength close to the Moon's surface = 1.6 N kg$^{-1}$)
NSAA-2019-M1-025: Given that $f(x) = \int_0^x (3+2t)^7\,dt$, what is the coefficient of $x^4$ in the expansion of f(x) in powers of x?
NSAA-2019-PHY-025: A student has one 300 Ω resistor and another resistor of resistance R. The student plots a graph of current I against potential difference V for the 300 Ω resistor and then for both resistors connected in parallel. [DIAGRAM: I-V graph, V axis 0-6 V, I axis 0-30 mA, two lines shown]. What is the resistance R?
NSAA-2019-M1-026: The three internal angles in a triangle are $\alpha$, $\beta$ and $\theta$, and $3\tan\alpha - 2\sin\beta = 2$, $5\tan\alpha + 6\sin\beta = 8$. What is the value of $\theta$ in degrees?
NSAA-2019-PHY-026: A light, vertical, copper wire of length 2.4 m and uniform cross-sectional area $2.0\times10^{-6}\text{ m}^2$ supports a load of mass 4.0 kg. The Young modulus of copper is $1.2\times10^{11}$ Pa. What is the strain energy in the wire? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume Hooke's law and constant cross-sectional area)
NSAA-2019-M1-027: Find the complete set of values of x for which $x^3 - 2x^2 - 7x - 4 > 0$.
NSAA-2019-PHY-027: The velocity-time graph is for an 80 kg person in a lift that is moving vertically upwards. [DIAGRAM: velocity-time graph, velocity axis 0-2.0 m/s, time axis 0-25 s, with point X marked on the graph]. What is the magnitude of the contact force between the person and the lift floor at the time corresponding to X? (g = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
